title: Mathematics in Machine learning
summary: The lesson explains how machine learning depends on mathematics, especially linear algebra, to convert raw data into numerical forms computers can process. It introduces core data structures, similarity measures, matrix operations, and singular value decomposition as the basis for efficient large-scale ML.
topics: Why machines need mathematics to process data
Linear algebra as the foundation of ML
Dot products in vectors and matrices
Why linear algebra matters in modern AI systems

title: Mathematics in machine learning
summary: The video explains why linear algebra is foundational to machine learning: raw data is converted into numerical objects, compared with similarity measures, and transformed efficiently with matrix operations. It then shows how SVD and matrix factorization help reduce dimensionality and recover structure in large datasets such as movie ratings, with direct relevance to modern neural networks and LLMs.
topics: Linear algebra as the language of data
Vectorization of images, text, audio, video, and structured data
How linear algebra supports neural networks and LLMs
